title: Number Systems, Signed Quantities, and Reliable Operations · Assumption Clinic
summary: Reconcile inventory gains, losses, temperatures, elevations, and account changes from mixed records. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a signed-quantity inventory reconciliation and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Classify natural, whole, integer, rational, irrational, and real numbers by the operations a model needs.
Use place value, scientific notation, and powers of ten to compare very large and very small quantities.
Model direction and change with signed numbers and explain each operation on a number line.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Rounding Precision evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Rounding Precision assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Round only at a justified reporting boundary while preserving sufficient working precision. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Compare plausible approaches against the decision, constraints, evidence quality, and cost of being wrong.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: math beginner number operations. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Rounding Precision, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a signed-quantity inventory reconciliation.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Rounding every intermediate value improves accuracy. This fails the Rounding Precision contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Recalculate the repaired case
steps.instruction: A measured quantity starts at 19 units, changes by −6 units, then by +2 units. Calculate the final quantity.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Preserve the signs and compute 19 − 6 + 2; compare with the starting scale.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Preserve the signs and compute 19 − 6 + 2; compare with the starting scale.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes units.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Rounding Precision applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Round only at a justified reporting boundary while preserving sufficient working precision.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The calculated quantity is accurate to the stated tolerance and carries its requested unit.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
